title: panicum crus-galli subsp. colonum
intro: Panicum crus-galli subsp. colonum, also known as tall wind grass or green foxtail, is a subspecies of the common cockspur grass that is native to Europe and Asia but has become naturalized in other parts of the world. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of P. crus-galli subsp. colonum include its ability to reduce fever, treat respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
